title: assessment.completed payload (v1)
description: Emitted when an assessment is graded. TWO producers publish the SAME subject with the SAME shape: the native grading worker (apps/assessment-service/src/workers/grading.worker.ts) and the vendor-result ingest (apps/assessment-service/src/lib/ingest-vendor-result.ts). The vendor path additionally carries `provider` (optional here). CRITICAL: `passed` is null while a human review is pending — this event NEVER encodes an automated adverse decision (GDPR Art.22).
x-consumers: candidate-service (advance application to ASSESSMENT stage, forward-only, never auto-rejects)
notification-service (raise HITL checkpoint when flagged + notify tenant admins; audit + webhook fan-out)

attemptId.description: Attempt id. For the vendor ingest path this is a synthetic attempt id.

applicationId.description: Resolved application to advance; null for a standalone assessment / when no application resolves.

passed.description: Pass/fail verdict, or NULL while a human review is pending (no auto-reject). Consumers treat null as "held for review".

needsReview.description: True when the grade needs a human look (pending open-ended items, low-confidence AI grade, or a marginal numeric result).

provider.description: Vendor ingest path only: provider key. Absent on the native grading path.
